C-ASTRAL Aerospace is a global market leader with an established reputation in the specialized, fixed-wing small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) manufacturing and services field.

The company is a global market leader with established reputation in the specialized, fixed wing small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) manufacturing and services field, with a specific focus on high productivity, endurance, surveying and remote sensing between the commercial UAS operators, larger institutional networks.

evidence.snippet: The company is a global market leader with established reputation in the specialized, fixed wing small Unmanned Aircraft Systems (UAS) manufacturing and services field, with a specific focus on high productivity, endurance, surveying and remote sensing between the commercial UAS operators, larger institutional networks.

C-Astral Aerospace, based in Slovenia (EU) designs high-endurance, blended wing body UAS since 2007, government and commercial use, with systems deployed in over 70 countries. The SQA2’s Red Dot Award highlights its innovative design and global impact.

readiness.notes: Field-proven systems deployed in 70+ countries with 120,000+ flight hours. Active spare parts quotation available.

unresolved_questions: C3P Software product page not fetched — unable to confirm if sold standalone or bundled-only, and exact feature set.
DYN Micro Datalink product page not fetched — datalink specs and standalone sales status unknown.
No NDAA/Blue UAS compliance information found; company is EU-based (Slovenia) so may not pursue US compliance listings.

fetch_requests.reason: Dedicated page for C3P software product; would clarify whether it is sold standalone (affecting ground_segment classification), feature set, and any compliance claims.
Dedicated page for DYN Micro Datalink; would confirm product specs, standalone vs. bundled sale, and strengthen communications category evidence.

tagline: Slovenian OEM producing fixed-wing and blended-wing ISR platforms (Bramor C4EYE, SQA eVTOL, ATLAS AS90X) and survey-grade airframes with 3+ hour endurance, plus stabilized EO/IR gimbals and ground-control software. Field-proven in European operations. NDAA and Blue UAS certification status unknown; no named US defense contracts in public record.
